category_title: Distracted students using technology or showing physical disengagement
minimal_context: The student is working independently on a computer-based project assignment to create a company letterhead in an elective class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please start working independently on your computer-based project assignment to create a company letterhead.

STUDENT: I need some clarity on this assignment. What are the directions?

TUTOR: Just read the prompt on the screen and see what you can do.

STUDENT: I don't get it. (Puts head on the desk)

TUTOR: Keep trying to work on the project.

STUDENT: (Lifts head, gets distracted, opens another website on the computer, and starts texting on a cell phone)

options.A: Checks the student's progress on the computer platform every Sunday night to see what mistakes the student is making and what project steps they are struggling with during independent work.
options.B: Publicly celebrates awards and improvements, such as reduced off-task behavior or increased project completion, as often as possible with the student during the elective class sessions.
options.C: Utilizes differentiated instruction, including guided practice, direct teaching, one-on-one instruction, and technology, alongside role play, games, group learning, peer tutoring, and a whatever-works approach for the computer-based project.
options.D: Provides an audio cue that sounds every minute, prompting the student to record whether they were on task (e.g., not texting) during the preceding interval by checking Yes or No on a sheet of paper at their desk.
